summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the sermon with a humorous but pointed distinction between being 'generous' and being 'gener-ish' (or 'jish'), a term he coined to describe giving that is self-serving, such as giving away leftovers or items one doesn't want. He challenged the congregation to move from a 'gener-ish' mindset to true, biblical generosity. The core of the message was that real generosity is sacrificial, motivated by love, and begins with seeing the needs around us. Pastor Travis grounded this call in the Christmas story, noting that God's ultimate generosity was giving His only Son, and the wise men responded with costly, worshipful gifts. He taught that generosity starts with our eyes—actively noticing opportunities to give—and is blessed by God. Using the stark example of Ananias and Sapphira from Acts, he warned that giving for the wrong reasons, like seeking recognition or manipulating outcomes, is not true generosity. He emphasized that we can be givers without being generous if our hearts and motives are wrong. True generosity is cheerful, sacrificial, and understands that everything we have belongs to God, making us stewards, not owners. He concluded with an urgent call from Romans 12:1 to offer our lives as living sacrifices, which is our true worship.
mainPoints: Generosity begins with the eyes: actively noticing needs and opportunities to give, which God blesses.
You can be a giver without being generous; true generosity comes from a right heart and motive, not from a desire for recognition or personal gain.
The purest form of generosity is sacrificial, understanding that everything belongs to God and we are merely stewards of His resources.

versesMentioned.text: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

When they saw the star, they were overjoyed. On coming to the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense and myrrh.
The generous will themselves be blessed, for they share their food with the poor.
With his wife’s full knowledge he kept back part of the money for himself, but brought the rest and put it at the apostles’ feet.

So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full. But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously. Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

As Jesus looked up, he saw the rich putting their gifts into the temple treasury. He also saw a poor widow put in two very small copper coins. 'Truly I tell you,' he said, 'this poor widow has put in more than all the others. All these people gave their gifts out of their wealth; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.'

Command those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant nor to put their hope in wealth, which is so uncertain, but to put their hope in God, who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment. Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share. In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 22:9 (NIV) - 'The generous will themselves be blessed, for they share their food with the poor.'
Monday.action: Actively look for one need or opportunity to be generous today, whether with time, a kind word, or a resource.
Monday.prayer: God, open my eyes today to see the needs around me. Give me a generous eye and a willing heart to act.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:7 (NIV) - 'Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.'
Tuesday.focus: Examining the heart of our giving.
Tuesday.action: Review a recent act of giving (time, money, etc.). Pray over your motives, asking God to purify your heart to give cheerfully for His sake.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, search my heart. Remove any selfishness, pride, or desire for recognition. Make me a cheerful giver who delights in You.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 21:3-4 (NIV) - 'Truly I tell you,' he said, 'this poor widow has put in more than all the others. All these people gave their gifts out of their wealth; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.'
Wednesday.action: Identify one area where you can give sacrificially this week—something that would genuinely 'cost' you in time, comfort, or resources.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, give me the heart of the widow. Help me to trust You so completely that I can give sacrificially, knowing You are my provider.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Timothy 6:18-19 (NIV) - 'Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share.'
Thursday.action: Take an inventory of a key resource God has entrusted to you (finances, a skill, your home). Plan one specific way to use it for His kingdom this month.
Thursday.prayer: God, everything I have is Yours. Help me to be a faithful and wise manager of all You've given me, using it for Your glory.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 6:3-4 (NIV) - 'But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret.'
Friday.action: Perform an anonymous act of generosity today. Do not tell anyone about it.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, let my giving be an act of worship done for Your eyes alone. May it be pure and pleasing to You.

Saturday.scripture: Romans 12:1 (NIV) - 'Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.'
Saturday.action: Spend time in prayer, consciously offering your entire self—your time, talents, and treasures—to God as a living sacrifice for the coming week.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, in view of Your great mercy, I offer my whole life to You again. Use me as a living sacrifice, and make me generous in every way, for this is my true worship.
